commit.message:
style: ruff format 修正 conftest.py + test_spells.py #1

Signed-off-by: tinyoddsthings <tinyoddsthings@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add full Web Push notification system

- Service worker (public/sw.js): handles push events and notification clicks
- usePushNotifications hook: registers SW, manages subscription state, keeps local 30s interval check as fallback
- POST/DELETE /api/notifications/subscribe: stores push subscriptions in Supabase
- POST /api/notifications/send-due: cron endpoint — sends timed reminders (at task time + 15 min early), all-day digest at 8 AM UTC, overdue digest at 9 AM UTC; deduplicates via notification_log; auto-removes expired subscriptions (410/404)
- vercel.json: cron every 5 minutes (requires Pro; use 0 8,9 * * * on Hobby)
- Dashboard bell: green when subscribed, click to toggle on/off
- Settings: Notifications section with enable/disable toggle + feature description
- Delete account: clears push_subscriptions on account deletion

Requires manual steps before use:
  1. Run SQL for push_subscriptions + notification_log tables in Supabase
  2. npx web-push generate-vapid-keys → add NEXT_PUBLIC_VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY, VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY, VAPID_SUBJECT, CRON_SECRET, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY to .env.local and Vercel

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: lat/lon proximity search replaces fragile city-string matching

Add GEO_COORDS table (50+ locations with lat/lon/radius_km) to
taxonomy_mapping.py. Fuzzy preprocessor now resolves location names
to coordinates alongside city lists. Coordinates are injected directly
into IntentResult in app.py (bypassing Gemini), persisted via session
merge, and used in CSSL as a Haversine distance filter.

Priority: lat/lon radius search > cities list > single city string.

'cheer camps in north york' now finds camps within 12km of North York
centre regardless of their city label (Toronto vs North York vs Thornhill).
Eliminates the need to maintain neighbourhood->city string mappings for
geographic search accuracy.

Also: geography reset in session_manager now clears stale lat/lon when
user broadens to province level.

Fix prediction calibration: shrinkage, compound probability, remove alert bypass

- Raise prediction cap from 75 to 500 (dedup/junk filters handle quality)
- Add overconfidence shrinkage: pull stated confidence toward 0.30 anchor
- Add compound probability detection: discount claims conditional on scenarios
  (e.g., Hormuz closure -> oil spike properly compounds P(scenario) * P(outcome))
- Cap evidence strength at 4 (base rate gets >= 20% weight always)
- Cap max output confidence at 0.85 (was 0.95)
- Add calibration rules to prompt: compound probability, base rates, range guidance
- Remove alert chain auto-prediction creation (was bypassing all calibration)
- Predictions now generated exclusively by the calibrated engine

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use write() instead of end() to keep socat bridge alive

sock.end() half-closes the TCP connection, causing socat to
propagate FIN through the vsock chain and tear down the pipe
before the enclave can send its response back.

Using write() keeps the connection open; the enclave responds
with socket.end(json) which closes the connection cleanly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix #185 infinite loop: zustand selector + questions memo + setInputSpec guard

Root cause: session-view subscribed to the entire session store (no
selector), so every setInputSpec/recordScore update re-rendered the
full component tree. If any child activity created new object refs
in effect deps (e.g. questions ?? []), the effect → store → re-render
cycle looped infinitely.

Three fixes:
1. session-view: use individual zustand selectors instead of full
   store destructure — prevents re-renders from inputSpec/score changes
2. mission-selector: useMemo for questions to stabilize the reference
3. setInputSpec: skip no-op updates (null→null, same ref)

feat(m4a,m4b): chaos test scripts + crash recovery integration tests (4.5) (#97)

* feat(m4a,m4b): chaos test scripts + crash recovery integration tests (4.5)

Add pluggable chaos test hooks for the E2E chaos framework:

  verification tests (4 stub Unimplemented + interference validation)
- chaos_test_policy.sh: M4b stateful crash recovery with cold-start
  bandit creation, arm selection, and RocksDB state persistence

Add 4 Rust integration tests for M4b crash recovery under load:
- Multi-experiment concurrent recovery (Thompson + LinUCB + cold-start)
- Kafka offset preservation across crash/restore cycle
- High-volume (2200+ rewards) crash recovery with learned preferences
- Recovery timing assertion (< 10s SLA, < 1s for 10 experiments)

34 policy tests pass, clippy clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add ACH transfers (Phase 4) - Real bank transfers via Plaid Transfer API

Features:
- plaid-initiate-transfer edge function (creates real ACH transfers)
- plaid-webhook edge function (handles transfer status updates)
- Automated deposit processing via webhooks
- Automated withdrawal processing via webhooks
- Enhanced InvestorCapital.jsx with real ACH flows
- Transaction history shows transfer IDs, settlement dates, status
- Database migration adds description and metadata to investor_capital
- Comprehensive documentation with testing checklist

Transfer Flow:
1. Investor initiates deposit/withdrawal
2. System creates Plaid authorization
3. System initiates ACH transfer (debit/credit)
4. Webhook receives status updates (pending → posted → settled)
5. Auto-processes when settled (updates balances, distributes capital)

Estimated Settlement:
- Deposits: 3-5 business days
- Withdrawals: 2-3 business days

commit.message:
Fix weekly A/X classification to compare Monday vs Friday

The A/X determination was incorrectly comparing full current week
range against full prior week range. Per ICT quarterly theory,
accumulation (A) is defined by Monday (Q1) being inside Friday's
(prior session) range. Now correctly compares monday.high/low
against friday.high/low while keeping PWH/PWL as full prior week
range for manipulation analysis.

commit.message:
Usa claude-3-5-sonnet-latest para evitar modelos depreciados

Problema:
- Todos os modelos com data específica foram depreciados
- claude-3-opus-20240229: deprecated, EOL em 05/01/2026 ❌
- claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022: não existe ❌
- claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620: não existe ❌
- Estamos em março/2026, modelos antigos não funcionam mais

Solução:
- Usa 'claude-3-5-sonnet-latest' ✅
- Aponta automaticamente para versão mais recente disponível
- Evita problemas de depreciação futuros
- Mantém alta precisão do Claude 3.5 Sonnet
- Sempre usa o modelo mais atualizado

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: replace mirror-clone with edge-propagation inpainting for watermark remover

The old mirror-clone algorithm failed to remove Gemini watermarks because
it copied from too far away and blended with watermarked pixels at edges.
New approach samples clean border strips adjacent to the patch, interpolates
via inverse-distance weighting, and injects calibrated noise to match texture.
Also auto-sizes patch (96px for ≤1024px images, 144px for larger).

commit.message:
fix: align VsockClient wire protocol with enclave app

Two mismatches:
1. VsockClient sent 4-byte length-prefixed JSON, but the enclave
   expects raw JSON — removed the length header on send and the
   4-byte strip on receive.
2. VsockClient sent { type: "key" } but the enclave dispatches on
   the "action" field — now maps type → action before sending.

Also normalizes enclave responses: the "key" handler returns raw
data without an ok/data wrapper, so VsockClient now wraps those
into the standard EnclaveResponse format.

plan: cell-zero bootstrap roadmap — 5 phases from syntax discovery to metacircular loop

Phase 1: Evolutionary syntax discovery (ce-s6y)
Phase 2: Clean rewrite of executor with eval-one semantics
Phase 3: Observer + content addressing + staleness
Phase 4: Distillation + shadow execution + promotion
Phase 5: Metacircular loop (cell-zero rewrites itself)

Current subzero/parser marked as scrap — wrong mental model.
Lean proofs and design docs preserved.

commit.message:
feat: add autonomous exploration loop (autoresearch.py)

Central orchestration module for autonomous specification curve exploration.
Three-phase architecture:
- Systematic (60%): deterministic gap-filling across spec space
- Hypothesis (30%): LLM-driven specs via findings detection bridge
- Robustness (10%): one-dimension-at-a-time variation from baselines

Includes 5 findings detectors (method divergence, circumstance sensitivity,
measure sensitivity, wide CIs, outliers) and synthesis checkpoints.

commit.message:
feat: add CEEY-aligned variables and IREH-O wealth index (27->33 circumstances)

Integrate variables from CEEY's Informe de Movilidad Social 2025 do-file
to enable direct comparability with official Mexican social mobility estimates.

New circumstance variables:
- father/mother_education_6: 6-category CEEY education scale (p43+p44)
- max_parent_education: max(father, mother) education -- CEEY primary variable
- floor_material_14: binary floor material (tierra vs otro, from p25)
- overcrowding_14: continuous hacinamiento ratio (p22/p23)
- wealth_index_origin: IREH-O via MCA (Burt method, per cohort, 19 assets + hacinamiento)

Other improvements:
- Correct state-to-region mapping using exact CEEY codes (R1-R5)
- MCA implementation uses prince library with count-index fallback
- 3 new strategy sets: CEEY-comparable, IREH-O solo, 6-cat edu + IREH-O
- Updated synthetic data DGP with new variables in income equation

commit.message:
Improve support for environment types and fix description of plugin (#1)

* Add support for WP_ENVIRONMENT_TYPE

New behaviour:

1. Resolve the environment type:
	1. Use the value of the `WP_ENV` constant if defined and palpable.
	2. Otherwise, use the value of the `wp_get_environment_type()` function if defined (introduced in WordPress 5.0.0).
	3. Otherwise, use `NULL`.
2. Filter the environment type with the following hook:
	* `roots/bedrock/disallow_indexing_environment_type`
3. Display the notice:
	1. If the environment type is palpable, display the notice with the filtered environment type.
	2. Otherwise, display a shorter notice that does not reference the environment type.

* Update Overview in README

Fixed the description of the plugin to indicate that it relies on the value of a `DISALLOW_INDEXING` constant and not `WP_ENV`. The latter constant only being used as a reference in an dashboard notice.

* Apply suggestion from @retlehs

Prefer fewer else statements and early returns.

See: https://github.com/roots/bedrock-disallow-indexing/pull/1#issuecomment-2608867335

* Update URL

* Drop function_exists check and escape environment type output

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
disown all background jobs in boot() so terminal exits cleanly

_auto_update and rom-checker were tracked background children — bash
waits for them before the script truly exits, keeping xterm/SSH open.
disown detaches them so the terminal closes as soon as boot() returns.
